title: Fixes in Imagenet training script
body:
* ngpus_per_node variable count fix
* dist_backend assertion added for nccl issue

files_modified.patch:
@@ -106,8 +106,12 @@ def main():
 
     if torch.cuda.is_available():
         ngpus_per_node = torch.cuda.device_count()
+        assert not (ngpus_per_node == 1 and args.dist_backend == "nccl"),\
+        "nccl backend requires GPU count>1, see https://github.com/NVIDIA/nccl/issues/103 perhaps use 'gloo'"
     else:
-        ngpus_per_node = 1
+        ngpus_per_node = 0
+        assert args.dist_backend != "nccl",\
+        "nccl backend does not work without GPU, see https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/distributed.html"
     if args.multiprocessing_distributed:
         # Since we have ngpus_per_node processes per node, the total world_size
         # needs to be adjusted accordingly

steps.operation: Fix GPU counting logic and add defensive assertions to prevent invalid NCCL distributed configurations
steps.reason: When CUDA is unavailable, setting `ngpus_per_node = 1` incorrectly implies a GPU exists and can cause the distributed setup to compute an incorrect `world_size` and attempt GPU/NCCL paths. Additionally, NCCL is not supported without GPUs and can hit known NCCL issues on single-GPU setups; explicit assertions fail fast with actionable guidance.
steps.side_effects: Running the script with `--dist-backend nccl` on CPU-only hosts or single-GPU machines will now raise an AssertionError early instead of failing later with harder-to-diagnose distributed/NCCL errors. Any downstream logic that assumes `ngpus_per_node >= 1` when CUDA is unavailable may change behavior (e.g., world_size calculation and process spawning under `--multiprocessing-distributed`).

step_plan.summary: Corrects `ngpus_per_node` when CUDA is unavailable and adds assertions to prevent unsupported/buggy NCCL distributed configurations (CPU-only and single-GPU cases).
